L1.area: Binary program loading, parsing, and validation

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the processing of program headers from binary program files. The addition of virtual address and memory size fields can overflow, leading to a panic when handling crafted input with malformed headers.

L3.area: ELF binary parsing, validation, and relocation
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the processing of ELF program headers where the addition of virtual address and memory size fields can overflow when virtual address mapping is enabled, causing a panic when handling a crafted eBPF program with malformed program headers.

ground_truth.reason: The ELF validator does not check for integer overflow when adding program header virtual address and memory size fields, causing a panic on crafted input with malformed program headers.
